title: BP: List Cleanup with Selective Record Filtering and Updating a Different List
description: Cleans and filters records from a Bright Pattern list based on defined conditions and updates the filtered records into a new list, with notification and optional file export.
longDescription:
This workflow automates list cleanup and selective record updating within Bright Pattern. It extracts records from a source list, applies conditional filtering using JSON-based rules, and uploads the filtered records into a different Bright Pattern list.

**Purpose and main functionality:**
• Extract records from a Bright Pattern list and its associated campaign
• Filter records based on dialling result and record status (for example, Connection Status = Not Reachable)
• Upload filtered records into a new Bright Pattern list using mapped primary keys
• Send a notification email upon successful upload
• Optionally export filtered records to Microsoft OneDrive or AWS S3 for backup or reporting

**Scheduling:**
• Runs on a configurable schedule
• Example: hourly on Mondays starting at 9:00 AM (Australia/Sydney timezone)
• Schedule is fully customisable during setup
benefits: Automates Bright Pattern list cleanup without manual exports or spreadsheets.
Ensures to move relevant records are moved in timely fashion using clear, rule-based filtering.
Provides the ability to reuse the list and retain the list rules, avoid duplication.
Provides visibility through regular email notifications and optional file exports.

howToSteps.title: Configure the schedule and trigger
howToSteps.description: Set the Start block to run on a schedule. You can configure it to run hourly, daily, or weekly. Example configuration: hourly on Mondays starting at 9:00 AM (Australia/Sydney). This schedule is fully customisable.

howToSteps.title: Extract records from Bright Pattern
howToSteps.description: Use the Bright Pattern - Get Records block to retrieve records from a selected Bright Pattern list. Set the operation to retrieve all records from the defined list and its associated campaign. Select the list visually using the dropdown.

howToSteps.title: Filter records using JSON rules
howToSteps.description:
Add a JSON Filter block to clean the fetched records. Configure the filter using list column names. Example filter:
[{"field":"Connection Status","operator":"equals","value":"Not Reachable"}]
This ensures only records matching the defined condition are passed to the next step.

howToSteps.title: Upload filtered records into a new list
howToSteps.description: Use the Bright Pattern - Upload Records block to insert the filtered records into a different Bright Pattern list. Map the primary key from the filtered data (for example, Phone Number) to the target list field (for example, Phone no.).

howToSteps.title: Send upload confirmation
howToSteps.description: Use the Office 365 Email block to notify recipients of the successful upload. Include details of the uploaded records in the email body.

howToSteps.title: Optional file export
howToSteps.description: Optionally add Microsoft OneDrive or AWS S3 blocks to upload the filtered records as a file for backup, auditing, or reporting purposes.
